entries.problem: When employees or contractors leave the company and their Okta access is cut off, their Sanity.io permissions are not automatically removed. The team needed a way to be notified of offboarding events for users with Sanity access and ideally automate the deprovisioning.
entries.solution: As an interim solution, a manual Lumos task/notification step was added to alert the Sanity admin (Brian) when a user is offboarded, so they can manually remove Sanity access. The admin was also added to the CC list on offboarding tickets. A longer-term automation was proposed using a Lumos webhook to Workato, which would call the Sanity API to retrieve the user ID, loop through their assigned roles, and remove them; access reviews would catch any missed users quarterly.

rawMessages.text:

Ideally I could be notified of anyone who is leaving when they have the Sanity permission and they are leaving the company, contractor or Fool.  Any ideas?

We may also be able to automate it which would be great but happy to improve in some way.

Hey Brian! I can cc you on offboarding request tickets to start off. Do you need names of the ones run from the last week or so?

As far as it being automated, I am less certain. I'm guessing by your question that it may auto-provision an account when assigned but doesn't auto de-provision. It's possible it can be edited within Okta settings - I will take a look but that might require Sys Admin Powers™

Yes! Can help on the automating side of this - we can build it into Lumos to send you (and anyone else) a notification that the user has been deprovisioned from Sanity in Okta. It'll come through as a Lumos task in Slack that you or someone from your team will need to acknowledge if that works for you Brian.

<@U0344PGEEBT> BTW this might be a good use case for Lumos to automate: Webhook from Lumos to Worakto from when user needs to be removed from sanity>

Workato function can call the Sanity API gets the user ID, loops the roles assigned to the user and removes the roles.

Access reviews ensures removed users one a quarterly basis.
<https://www.sanity.io/docs/http-reference/access-api#k8e41778f1ef2>
It is <http://Sanity.io|Sanity.io> and I just periodically keep an eye on it including checking Chart Hop
^ is there a function to remove, disable or delete a user in the console dashboard UI what action do you take to term users ?
I bet there is, I'll have to look into it.
Okay curious to know this, thanks!

<@W0184HUHRBL> I added a manual step here for this to notify you through Lumos to remove access for the user when termed. Anyone else that has access to do this you might to notify also outside of you to do the task and for redundancy?


<@U0344PGEEBT> I saved you the Lumos admin task here, done

entries.problem: User needed an API key to make requests to Claude's API for a proof-of-concept project, and also wanted access to a Slack bot for API access.
entries.solution: API keys for Claude can be obtained through the internal keyring portal at https://orion.fool.com/keyring. User was directed there and was able to create a key for Claude API access.

Tagging you, <@WTDJUKU77>, since I'm not sure who else would handle the Claude API access.

entries.problem: A new contractor was not added to the correct Confluence groups during onboarding because the onboarding form submitted the wrong role ('Fool.com Freelance Writer' instead of 'Fool.com AI Content Writer'). Additionally, the contractor onboarding form's list of available roles was out of sync with the roles available in Okta.
entries.solution: The contractor was manually added to the two required Confluence groups ('Confluence-Contractors-Fool.com-Writer' and 'confluence-contractors-fool.com-ai_assisted'). Their role in Okta was updated to 'Fool.com AI Content Writer', with Lumos syncing the remaining permissions automatically. The root cause (mismatch between onboarding form roles and Okta roles) was identified for a follow-up fix to the onboarding form.

We ask that the onboarding duplicate the permissions that <mailto:jon***@foolcontractors.com|jon***@foolcontractors.com> has so I'm not sure why it is leaving off these Confluence permissions.
Ok cool. I'll work on getting Brendan setup now, then create a ticket to address the onboarding flow.

I've just updated Brendan's role in Okta to `<http://Fool.com|Fool.com> AI Content Writer` and Lumos will assign any missing perms on the next sync. That plus the work Brian is doing right now should get Brendan sorted.

entries.problem: Tableau access requests in Lumos were being routed to the wrong team (DDGS) and needed to be redirected to the BI team. Additionally, Snowflake-US Lumos entitlement integration was causing errors and needed to be removed, keeping only user creation/disable functionality.
entries.solution: Tableau requests in Lumos were updated to route to the BI team. For Snowflake-US, the Lumos entitlement integration was requested to be removed, leaving only user creation/disable triggers to avoid recurring errors while RBAC is being refined.

Got it, Tableau requests are now updated to go to BI! Thanks, Dominic!

<@U0344PGEEBT> additionally, for Snowflake - US:  Can we remove the Lumos entitlement integration and just have it trigger user creation/disable?

Entitlements don't work for the integration, and we don't want them to at this point, and it keeps kicking back Lumos errors.  We will probably explore this, later, but we are working to refine/redefine our RBAC in Snowflake so it isn't always a straightforward entitlement flow

entries.problem: A GitHub Actions workflow was sending daily OOO (out-of-office) reminders for each day someone was away, causing notification spam. For example, a 10-week sabbatical would trigger daily reminders for the entire duration across three Slack channels (investing-tech-team, security-team, product-tech-team).
entries.solution: The GitHub Actions workflow was modified to send only weekly OOO updates instead of daily reminders for the three specified teams. Conditional logic was added to the automation to change the notification frequency for those channels.

rawMessages.text:

In the following channels (<#G0K6RDU03>, <#C08CNQUT459>, <#C0358CCP1DY>) Github actions drops a reminder about OOO from the team calendars. However, it drops a reminder for every day when someone is OOO. So if someone is OOO for 1 week, a reminder is dropped everyday of that week.
For example, Julie Clarenbach is on sabbatical for then next 10 weeks. Anyway to not get a reminder everyday for the next 10 weeks? Can this be just the first day of the week ?
Hi Priya, I'll look into this.

<@U02BDNYT4G5> The GitHub action is a single workflow being applied to all the different teams who receive OOO notifications. I'll need a little more time to connect with the team to discuss splitting or recreating the workflow.
all those 3 channels - would prefer to have the OOO be notified at the beginning of the week or beginning of the OOO - but not everyday of the OOO
Understood! Which reminds me, could you list the channels in plain text? Since they're private, I'm unable to see what they are when Slack channel links are used.

Morning Priya! We discussed this as a team and think there's a good way to create conditional actions in the automation that will change up how the posts go out for the 3 teams that requested it. I'm gonna spin up a card for this and will post updates there!
Thank you!
@chris.yong created a [System] Service request SYSADM-3672 OOO Notification GH Action Modification

the channels are
1. investing-tech-team
2. security-team
3. product-tech-team
Thanks!

<@U02BDNYT4G5> Thanks for your patience, Chevon and I have just got done modifying our Action to provide only weekly updates for the 3 teams you noted above! We'll be able to see on Monday if our changes run smoothly.

entries.problem: Departing systems admin needed to transfer Brandfolder account ownership to a colleague before their last day, but the colleague was listed as OOO and needed to accept an admin invite first.
entries.solution: The departing admin sent an admin invite to the new owner via Brandfolder. The new owner (who was actually available) located the invite email and clicked through it to accept, which granted her admin access. The departing admin confirmed the invite was accepted and proceeded to follow up with Brandfolder reps to complete the ownership transfer.
